message.content: {"scene": "A vast server room stretches into darkness, its ceiling lost somewhere above a forest of blinking towers that hum like a slow, mechanical tide. The small blue creature crouches before a single glowing screen, one hand raised mid-reach toward a refresh button it has already pressed a thousand times, the other hand resting flat on the cold floor as though checking whether the ground is still there. Beside the screen sits a plate of food gone lukewarm, a cheerful sticky note curling at its corner like a tiny flag planted by someone who still believes in morning, and a calendar whose weeks are buried under so many penciled hash marks they have become a kind of texture rather than time. Somewhere in the blinking dark, an enormous boulder balances on a stack of smaller boulders, all of them balanced on the creature's back without touching it yet, close enough to feel the shadow. The creature's mouth is open, rehearsing a sentence that has not yet found its moment, a shape of sound waiting for daylight to make it real. Mood: the exhaustion of a person who can see exactly what is happening to them and has not yet decided whether knowing is enough. Palette: deep server-blue, ash gray, the warm amber of a refrigerator light, one reluctant thread of dawn gold at the far edge.", "title": "The Rehearsed Sentence"}
